title: Method for producing a membrane-nanowire composite
abstract: The present invention relates to a method for producing a membrane-nanowire composite comprising a polymeric membrane and metal nanowires. Such nanowires may be structured and used in the production of electronic or other devices. The present membrane-nanowire composites are particularly suited for use in low-temperature bonding and/or sealing applications.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Method for producing a membrane-nanowire composite comprising a polymeric membrane and metal nanowires, said method comprising the steps of (a) providing a porous polymeric membrane; (b) submerging the porous polymeric membrane in an electrolyte bath comprising an aqueous solution of ions of a metal; (c) then depositing said metal until a thin metal film is obtained on one side of the polymeric membrane and further until a thin film is obtained on one side of the polymeric membrane; (d) subsequently removing the polymeric membrane from the electrolyte bath; and (e) in the following swelling the polymer with a solvent, thereby obtaining the membrane-nanowire composite. 2. Method according to claim 1, characterized in that the polymeric membrane comprises a polymer selected from the group consisting of cellulose acetate, nitrocellulose, cellulose esters, polysulfone (PSF), polyether sulfone (PES), polyacrylonitrile (PAN), polyamide (PA), polyimide (PI), polyethylene (PE), polypropylene (PP), polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF), polyvinylchloride (PVC), polycarbonate (PC), polyurethane (PU), polystyrene (PS), and any blend of any of these. 3. Method according to claim 1 or claim 2, characterized in that the pores of the polymeric membrane have a diameter of at least 5 nm and of at most 500 nm. 4. Method according to any one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that the polymeric membrane has a thickness of at most 500 pm. 5. Method according to any or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that the metal is selected from the group consisting of silver, gold, indium, zinc, copper, palladium, platinum, titanium, zirconium, molybdenum, scandium or any blend of any of these. 6. Method according to any one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that the metal is selected from the group consisting of silver, gold, copper, platinum and any blend of any of these. 7. Method according to any or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that the metal is copper. 8. Method according to any or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that the electrolyte bath comprises a strong acid. 9. Method according to any or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that the electrolyte bath comprises sulfuric acid. 10. Method according to any one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that in step (c) the metal is electrochemically deposited. 11. Method according to any one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that the solvent is selected from the solvents to which the respective polymer comprised in the polymeric membrane shows reduced chemical resistance. 12. Method according to any one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that the method further comprises the step of (f) structuring the membrane-nanowire composite. 13. Method according to any one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that the method further comprises the step of (g) depositing the membrane-nanowire composite on a substrate; and (h) removing the polymeric membrane, thereby obtaining a pad of metal nanowires on the substrate. 14. Method according to any one or more of the preceding claims, characterized in that the method further comprises the step of (i) aligning two pads of metal nanowires on the substrate on top of each other so as to allow for interpenetration of the metal nanowires; and 

 (k) subsequently pressing the two pads together, thereby bonding the two pads together.
